desc: Paphos blends archaeological sites, resort beaches, and low-key harbor life on Cyprus's southwest coast. It works well for travelers who want sun and sea plus easy access to ancient mosaics, sea caves, and wine villages in the Troodos foothills.
hls: UNESCO mosaics and ruins at Kato Paphos Archaeological Park
Harbor promenade with castles, tavernas, and sunset views
Sea caves, Akamas Peninsula trails, and Blue Lagoon boat trips
Mild winter climate compared with much of Europe
Reliable base for beach-and-history hybrid trips
Direct Gatwick flights in many seasons

todo.name: Kato Paphos Archaeological Park
todo.desc: Arrive at opening time to see mosaics before heat builds, and wear a hat because shade is sparse across the larger villa compounds.

todo.name: Paphos Castle and harbor walk
todo.desc: Do the harbor promenade in late afternoon, then climb the castle near sunset when temperatures ease and the marina light is best.

todo.name: Tombs of the Kings
todo.desc: Allow 60-90 minutes for the full site and bring water, as paths are exposed and the nearest cafés are outside the main gate.

todo.name: Coral Bay swim day
todo.desc: Buses from Kato Paphos are frequent but crowded by late morning in summer; arrive before 10:30 to secure loungers and calmer water.

todo.name: Akamas Peninsula jeep or boat tour
todo.desc: Book licensed operators 2-3 days ahead in peak season and confirm pickup points, as many tours depart from Latchi rather than central Paphos.

todo.name: Aphrodite's Rock stop
todo.desc: Visit around sunrise or sunset for safer road-side parking and softer light, and use water shoes if you plan to enter the pebly shoreline.

todo.name: Old Town (Ktima) food evening
todo.desc: Reserve meze restaurants for Friday-Sunday nights and use a short taxi uphill from the harbor to avoid the steep return walk.

todo.name: Troodos winery village loop
todo.desc: A rental car is most practical; start before 09:00 to combine Omodos and at least one winery tasting without rushing mountain driving after dark.

wifi.note: Generally dependable in hotels and aparthotels; many beachfront venues also provide stable backup connections.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection.
prac.lang: Greek, English widely used in tourism
prac.visa: Cyprus is in the EU but not Schengen; many non-EU visitors (including UK/US) can visit visa-free for short stays (commonly up to 90 days).
alerts: July-August heat and UV levels are intense, with limited shade at coastal ruins; schedule archaeological visits for opening hours and rest midday.
Akamas Blue Lagoon boat and jeep tours can be cancelled by sea state or wildfire restrictions, so keep a flexible backup day.
Direct UK flights and family resorts price up quickly for school-holiday weeks; book flights and beachfront stays 8-12 weeks ahead.

sweet: April to June and late September to October are sunny, warm, and less crowded than peak midsummer.
